title: block-file basename must equal config block_kinds[].canonical_id (undocumented usability constraint)
body: pi-context substrate-discovery functions (buildIdIndex in project-sdk.ts:615+ / availableBlocks in project-sdk.ts:53+ / projectState in project-sdk.ts:404+) scan .project/*.json files and map each file BASENAME to the matching canonical_id declared in config.block_kinds[]. If basename and canonical_id are misaligned (e.g. config declares canonical_id: "issues" but the file is .project/my-issues.json), the file is INVISIBLE to substrate-discovery: buildIdIndex skips it; availableBlocks does not list it; projectState does not count it. The file remains a valid JSON file on disk but is operationally orphaned — author intent silently dropped. Severity: usability constraint, not silent corruption (no errors, just missing-from-discovery). Surfaced during FGAP-026 phase 1.2 Section C.2.c retroactive runtime demo at HEAD e4ad4aa when constructing a custom-substrate fixture (subagent hit the constraint when initial blockName/canonical_id pair did not align). Constraint is not stated explicitly in CLAUDE.md or in block-api.ts source comments — emerges from the way blockFilePath(cwd, blockName) at block-api.ts:47 constructs <blockName>.json AND project-sdk discovery uses bk.canonical_id as the lookup key against the basename. Authors / agents writing custom substrates must follow the convention or substrate operations will not see their files. Resolution paths: (a) document the convention in CLAUDE.md under Project Blocks section (already added in same change-set landing this issue); (b) optionally add a runtime check in writeBlock that warns when blockName does not match any declared canonical_id; (c) optionally surface as a project-validate finding for orphaned block files.
resolved_by: Disposition: constraint satisfied by Phase 6.3 (context-init) design — onboarding prompts for substrate dir name + block-kind selection (canonical_id/prefix/display_name) so user never hand-authors config; basename/canonical_id binding is realized by workflow construction (context-install reads context-init-produced config and creates files matching declared canonical_ids) rather than by runtime check. No separate enforcement arc warranted; Phase 6.3 scope description updated in canonical sub-phase analysis + MEMORY.md current-focus to codify block-kind selection as part of the init prompts. Filed-then-closed in same arc per dispute over premise (basename mismatch presupposes user hand-edits substrate, but onboarding flow makes hand-editing unnecessary).
